Home.title: Heritage meets Innovation.
Home.desc: Sankofa International School delivers a deeply enriching, world-class curriculum in Hawassa, Ethiopia. We bridge the gap between profound cultural wisdom and cutting-edge global readiness, preparing students to lead with unyielding integrity.
Home.ethosDesc: We do not just prepare students for exams; we prepare them for the real world. By integrating elite STEM facilities with a profound respect for the arts and humanities, we craft well-rounded visionaries.
Home.program1Desc: Fostering boundless curiosity in a play-based, nurturing environment.
Home.program2Desc: Building core competencies alongside unbreakable ethical foundations.
Home.program3Desc: Rigorous university preparation featuring advanced STEM tracks.
Home.bentoFeature1Desc: Integrating an unyielding code of ethics, inspired by Ethiopian roots, into every facet of our daily curriculum.
Home.bentoFeature2Desc: State-of-the-art facilities equipped with advanced robotics, coding, and physical science resources.
Home.bentoFeature3Desc: Our 15+ international partnerships ensure students receive a completely borderless education.
Home.bentoFeature4Desc: A fully realized human requires physical vitality and creative expression. Our expansive sports complexes and fine arts programs nurture the soul and body alike.
Home.bentoText: Every extracurricular activity is carefully mapped to our core values. We ensure that whether a student is on the field or in the lab, they are operating with integrity, respect, and a drive for excellence.
Home.joinDesc: Applications for the 2026 Academic Year are now open.
Home.step1Desc: Submit an online inquiry form and download our comprehensive 2026 academic prospectus.
Home.step2Desc: Schedule a guided tour of our Hawassa campus to experience our facilities firsthand.
Home.step3Desc: Students complete age-appropriate academic and behavioral assessments.
Home.step4Desc: Family interview with the Head of School, followed by final registration.

About.title: Honoring Heritage. Inspiring Futures.
About.sub: Sankofa International School was established to provide an uncompromising educational environment in Hawassa. We believe that to build a vision for tomorrow, we must reach back and embrace our cultural roots.
About.philosophyText: Derived from the Akan people of West Africa, 'Sankofa' is symbolized by a bird reaching back to retrieve a valuable egg from its back. It teaches us that the past is a rich repository of wisdom. In Hawassa, we apply this philosophy by grounding our students in traditional Ethiopian values of community, respect, and deep integrity, while equipping them with modern, globally competitive academic tools.
About.missionText: To cultivate a community of critical thinkers and ethical leaders who excel academically, operate with unyielding integrity, and proudly honor their heritage.
About.visionText: To be recognized as East Africa’s premier educational institution, bridging the gap between historical African wisdom and futuristic technological readiness.
About.valuesText: Our culture is governed by four central pillars: Integrity (Haqqa), Community (Sidaama), Innovation, and Academic Rigor (Excellence).

Academics.heroDesc: Our academic framework is built on two pillars: rigorous global standards and an understanding of our cultural inheritance. We operate as an English-medium institution utilizing world-class frameworks.
Academics.stage1Desc: Serving children aged 3 to 5. We use play-based and inquiry-driven models to help toddlers learn motor skills, early phonics, spatial awareness, and creative expression.
Academics.stage2Desc: Integrating mathematics, sciences, English, and Amharic. Our primary school curriculum balances core intellectual training with ethical modeling, physical fitness, and heritage arts.
Academics.stage3Desc: Intensive university preparation. Students select specialized paths in either Advanced STEM (Sciences & Math) or Liberal Arts & Humanities, ensuring top-tier placement.
Academics.stemDesc: All secondary prep students undergo rigorous training in computational thinking. Our computer science laboratories teach coding (Python & JavaScript), robotics, and data science principles from Grade 7 onward.
Academics.artsDesc: Sankofa's arts program is deeply rooted. Students study traditional Ethiopian musical instruments, classical paintings, pottery, and cultural literature alongside global Western masterworks.
Academics.assessmentDesc: Our grading matrix complies with Cambridge Assessment standards. We measure progress through holistic projects, written analysis, presentations, and rigorous term-end formal exams.

Admissions.heroDesc: We welcome families who value academic excellence, character integrity, and traditional heritage. Applications for the upcoming academic year are reviewed on a rolling basis.
Admissions.step1Desc: Complete our quick digital inquiry form and download our comprehensive digital admissions pack.
Admissions.step2Desc: Book an appointment to tour our modern Hawassa campus and discuss requirements with counselors.
Admissions.step3Desc: Prospective students participate in age-appropriate academic, social, and behavioral assessments.
Admissions.step4Desc: A formal parent interview with the Head of School, followed by final seat registration.
Admissions.tuitionNote: * Note: Tuition does not include uniforms, textbooks, daily bus transportation within Hawassa, or external exam registration fees (Pearson/IGCSE).
Admissions.scholarshipDesc: We offer a merit-based scholarship program for exceptionally talented students in the Sidama Region who demonstrate financial need.
Admissions.faq1Q: What is the primary language of instruction?
Admissions.faq1A: English is the primary language of instruction across all grades. Amharic is taught as a mandatory second language to preserve local cultural fluency.
Admissions.faq2Q: Are there admissions assessments for Pre-K?
Admissions.faq2A: Early Years assessments are play-based and informal, focusing on motor skills development and general behavioral metrics.
Admissions.faq3A: We are approved by the Ethiopian Ministry of Education and offer international Pearson Edexcel IGCSE and GCE A-Level pathways.

Portal.title: Student Portal
Portal.note: Contact the IT Helpdesk if you have misplaced or forgotten your access key.
Portal.uplinkSuccessDesc: Your physical file has been cataloged and routed to the department supervisor.

Calendar.title: Academic Calendar.
Calendar.sub: Track session dates, examinations, administrative milestones, and community holidays for the 2026/2027 academic year.

Footer.schoolDesc: Committed to providing an uncompromising international academic standard, deeply rooted in the moral core of our Ethiopian heritage.
